new_classes.label_en: other still wine of fresh grapes (no PDO/PGI), actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol, in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 96)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.), tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol, in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 29 96)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing neither a protected designation of origin (PDO) nor a protected geographical indication (PGI), with an actual alcoholic strength by volume exceeding 15 % vol, presented in containers of more than 2 litres, classified under CN 2204 29 96. Classification criteria follow Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22; the non-volatile constituents of the total dry extract (within the meaning of Additional Note 4(A)) include sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four main discriminating criteria for CN 2204 29 96: (1) wine of fresh grapes, expressed via the parent class WineOfFreshGrapes; (2) absence of PDO/PGI, expressed via complement restrictions on the corresponding quality classes; (3) container volume > 2 L, expressed via the LargeContainer part restriction; and (4) actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol, expressed via the decimalRange restriction on alcoholicStrengthByVolumePercent and a someValuesFrom on AlcoholicStrengthByVolumeQuality. The TotalDryMatterQuality restriction reflects the Additional Note 4(A) reference, capturing that dry extract thresholds (including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts) are relevant classification criteria. However, the precise numerical thresholds from Additional Notes 4 and 5 (e.g. specific g/L values for total dry extract categories) are not fully formalised as data range restrictions because the exact threshold values for the '96' subcode are not specified in the provided legal note text — they are referenced to the additional notes without spelling out the values. This represents the main coverage gap (~25%).
